core.title: Core Questions
questions: Why do you want to study in the US?
Why this university?
Why this course/major?
Tell me about yourself.
What are your career goals?
How will this degree help you?
Why not study in India?
What will you do after graduation?
Why should we give you this visa?
How did you learn about this university?
What other universities did you apply to?
Why did you choose this university over others?
What do you know about the university?
What do you know about the city/state where your university is located?

why_us.title: Why US/Buffalo
questions: Why the USA specifically?
Why not Canada, UK, or Australia?
Why Buffalo? It's cold there.
Why not a more well-known city like New York or California?
What do you know about Buffalo's weather?
Are you prepared for the cold climate?
What specific opportunities does Buffalo offer for your field?
How will studying in Buffalo benefit your career more than studying elsewhere?

career.title: Career Goals & OPT
questions: What are your short-term and long-term career goals?
Do you plan to work in the US after graduation?
What is OPT? Tell me about it.
Do you plan to apply for OPT?
Will you return to India after your studies?
What job do you want after graduation?
Do you have any job offers?
How will your degree help you in your career?
What companies do you want to work for?
Why do you need a master's degree for your career?
Can't you achieve your goals with your current degree?
What if you don't get a job in the US?
Are you planning to apply for H1B?
Do you have plans to immigrate to the US permanently?

academics.title: Academics
questions: What did you study in your bachelor's?
Why did you choose this major?
What was your GPA/percentage?
Tell me about your academic background.
What subjects did you like most?
What research have you done?
Tell me about your final year project.
Why do you want to pursue a master's degree?
What courses will you take?
Who are the professors you want to work with?
What is your thesis/research topic?
How is this program different from your bachelor's?

financial.title: Financial Questions
questions: How will you finance your education?
Who is your sponsor?
What does your father do?
What does your mother do?
What is your family's annual income?
Do you have any loans?
How much does your program cost?
Can you show me your financial documents?
Do you have any scholarships?
Why didn't you get a scholarship?
How much money do you have in your bank?
Can you afford the full cost of attendance?
What if your funds run out?
Who will support you financially?
Do you have siblings? Are they studying?

personal.title: Personal Background
questions: Tell me about your family.
Where are you from?
What does your city/hometown do?
Are you married?
Do you have a girlfriend/boyfriend?
What do you do in your free time?
What are your hobbies?
Have you traveled abroad before?
Where have you traveled?
Do you have relatives in the US?
What languages do you speak?
Why are you going alone? (if applicable)

ties.title: Ties to India
questions: Why will you come back to India?
What ties do you have to India?
Do you have property in India?
What will you do when you return?
Do you have a job offer in India?
Why wouldn't you stay in the US?
Your family is in India, what about that?
What opportunities does India offer you?
How will you contribute to India after your education?
Do you plan to settle in the US?

work.title: Work Experience
questions: Do you have any work experience?
Tell me about your job/internship.
What did you learn from your work?
Why do you want to study if you have work experience?
Why did you leave your job?
What was your role?
How will this program help you advance in your career?
What projects did you work on?
Who was your employer?
How long did you work there?

challenging.title: Challenging Questions
questions: You've been rejected before. Why should we approve you now?
Your English seems weak. How will you manage?
Your grades are low. How will you succeed?
This is an expensive program. Is it worth it?
Why do you think you deserve this visa?
What if you don't get a job after graduation?
How do I know you won't overstay your visa?
Why should I believe you'll return to India?
You're going to a lesser-known university. Why?
What makes you different from other applicants?
Why did you take a gap year?
What did you do during your gap year?
Why the gap between your studies?

program.title: Program Specifics
questions: How long is your program?
When does your program start?
What is the duration of your course?
Is this a STEM program?
What is STEM OPT?
Tell me about your curriculum.
What specialization will you choose?
Will you do a thesis or non-thesis track?
What kind of research opportunities are available?
Do you have any teaching assistantship?
What facilities does your university have for your program?

followup.title: Common Follow-ups
questions: Can you elaborate on that?
Why do you say that?
How does that relate to your goals?
Can you give me an example?
What do you mean by that?
How will you handle that?
Are you sure about that?
What if that doesn't work out?
Can you be more specific?
How do you plan to achieve that?
What's your backup plan?
Have you thought about the challenges?
How will you overcome those obstacles?
What research have you done on this?
Can you prove that?

situational.title: Situational Questions
questions: What will you do if you get homesick?
How will you adapt to American culture?
What if your roommate has different habits?
How will you manage your time?
What if you face discrimination?
How will you handle academic pressure?
What if you fail a course?
How will you manage your finances?
What if you get sick?
How will you make friends?
What if your visa is rejected?
How do you handle stress?
What will you do during holidays?
How will you stay connected with family?
What if you don't like your program?
How will you deal with culture shock?
What if you don't get along with your advisor?
How will you balance studies and personal life?
